title: Parliamentary Activity - Perran Moon MP
meta_description: A record of Perran Moon's Parliamentary Activity, working for the people of Camborne, Redruth and Hayle in Westminster.
headings: h2: Perran's spoken and written contributions working for you in Westminster
text_excerpt: Parliamentary Activity - Perran Moon MP Skip to main content Hit enter to search or ESC to close Close Search Menu Menu Spoken Contributions 155 Written Questions 198 PARLIAMENTARY ACTIVITIES Perran's spoken and written contributions working for you in Westminster Recent Contributions Here are my recent Parliamentary contributions. See Older Contributions Written Question: Department for Business and Trade - Pay (20/10/2025) To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to help support people who were underpaid wages by their former employers. See More Education: Children with SEND - Mainstream Schools (20/10/2025) A statement regarding the adversarial nature of the SEND system for all stakeholders and a move to focus on inclusion as a key measure of success. CLICK BELOW TO READ MORE See More ENGLISH DEVOLUTION AND COMMUNITY EMPOWERMENT BILL COMMITTEE (Day 2 - 16/10/2025) Please click here for a collection of statements and questions made by Perran Moon on Day 2 of the English Devolution Bill Committee. See More Written Question: Department for Business and Trade - Funerals: Small Businesses (15/10/2025) To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what support her Department is providing small independent funeral directors in response to market pressures from large direct cremation providers? See More ENGLISH DEVOLUTION AND COMMUNITY EMPOWERMENT BILL COMMITTEE (Day 1 - 14/10/2025) Please click here for a collection of statements and questions made by Perran Moon on Day 1 of the English Devolution Bill Committee. See More Bovine Tuberculosis Control and Badger Culling (13/10/2025) A speech in a Westminster Hall debate following on from a petition called "End the Badger cull and adopt other approaches to bovine TB control". The petition received 389 signatures from Camborne, Redruth and Hayle. See More Written Question: Treasury - Financial Ombudsman Service: Standards (13/10/2025) To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps she is taking to tackle the backlog of cases at the Financial Ombudsman Service. See More Written Question: Treasury - Life Insurance (13/10/2025) To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what regulatory safeguards are in place to protect consumers from excessive premium increases following the transfer of life insurance policy liabilities. See More Written Question: Treasury - Life Insurance (13/10/2025) To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps her Department is taking to regulate the pricing of legacy life insurance policies following provider transfers. See More Housing, Communities and Local Government Questions (13/10/2025) A question asking for reassurances that unleashing the Cornish Celtic Tiger and maximising our economic potential is not reliant on Cornwall joining a Mayoral Combined Authority. CLICK BELOW TO READ MORE. See More Written Question: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs - Cats and Dogs: Smuggling (08/10/2025) To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what will be in the terms of the Carr Hill review. See More Written Question: Department for Education - Children: Community Development (23/09/2025) To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to improve community (a) understanding and (b) acceptance of (i) residential and (ii) support services for vulnerable children (A) in the community and (B) in the criminal justice system. See More Written Question: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology - Animal Experiments: Regulation (19/09/2025) To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment her Department has made of the potential merits of banning animal testing. See More Written Question: Department of Health and Social Care - General Practitioners: Finance (19/09/2025) To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what will be in the terms of the Carr Hill review.
